config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap jadi batas utama karena tanpa perlindungan yang kuat terhadap individu, kepentingan masyarakat mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan penindasan. Di Indonesia kita sering melihat alasan “demi kepentingan umum” dipakai secara longgar, padahal dampaknya justru merugikan orang kecil. Kepentingan sosial itu penting, tetapi harus dicapai tanpa mengorbankan hak dasar warga.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society's interests should automatically come first, because in the United States that idea has too often been used to excuse government overreach and violations of civil liberties. At the same time, individual rights cannot be treated as absolute in every case, since public health, safety, and basic order sometimes require limits. The key issue is not choosing society over the individual, but proving that any restriction is necessary, narrow, and accountable. If we lose strong individual rights, "the common good" can become a convenient label for abuse.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu secara umum. Dari pengalaman saya melihat Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” sering dipakai untuk menekan warga kecil, padahal yang kuat justru makin diuntungkan, jadi saya tidak percaya prioritas seperti itu aman tanpa batas yang sangat ketat. Memang ada situasi tertentu seperti kesehatan publik atau keamanan yang butuh pembatasan, tetapi itu harus benar-benar sempit, perlu, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Kalau hak individu dianggap nomor dua, sangat mudah bagi negara atau mayoritas untuk mengaburkan penindasan sebagai kebaikan bersama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should have blanket priority over the individual, because in the United States that framing has often been used to justify overreach by government and by majorities. Public health and safety can justify limits, but only when they are narrow, necessary, and subject to real oversight; otherwise “the common good” becomes a slogan for trampling unpopular people. I also disagree with the idea that individual rights are somehow a luxury that society can set aside whenever it wants, because rights are what protect minorities and dissenters from being crushed by the majority. The better standard is not society first or individual first, but whether a restriction is genuinely justified and does not go further than needed.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu secara umum. Dari pengalaman melihat Indonesia, dalih “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk melegitimasi kebijakan yang berat sebelah, sementara warga kecil yang menanggung akibatnya, jadi saya tidak percaya pada prioritas yang memberi ruang terlalu besar pada negara atau mayoritas. Memang ada pengecualian untuk kesehatan publik, keamanan, atau keadaan darurat, tetapi pembatasan itu harus sangat sempit, jelas alasannya, dan bisa diawasi ketat. Kalau tidak, “kepentingan sosial” mudah berubah menjadi alasan halus untuk menekan suara yang lemah dan mengabaikan hak dasar.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think the interests of society should take blanket priority over the rights of the individual. From an American perspective, that phrase has too often been a cover for government overreach and for majorities to steamroll unpopular people, especially when they can hide behind words like public good or common good. I do think there are real cases where rights must be limited for public health, safety, or emergency response, but those limits have to be narrow, necessary, and tightly accountable. If society is allowed to outrank the individual by default, then the rights that protect minorities, dissenters, and ordinary people lose their force exactly when they are most needed.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
